page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, yielding an 8 GB per vCPU ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz, the instance utilizes the x86_64 architecture with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1619.48 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1719.0. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3320.67 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3828.0. Memory bandwidth is also in the lower tier, measuring 29.09 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 21.83 GB/sec for large-block writes. Despite these limitations, the instance demonstrates average performance in cryptographic tasks, static web serving at 6.77 GB/sec, and small-model LLM inference, processing 391.53 tokens per second for a 135M model prompt.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highmem-4 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive multi-core parallel processing. The inclusion of AMX and AVX-512 instructions provides qualitative hardware acceleration for specific vector and integer operations without the cost of a dedicated GPU. This server is best suited for workloads such as small-scale database operations, in-memory caching, static web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 2 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a 105 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU execution and cryptographic processing, alongside average static web serving throughput of 6.77 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, which assist with vector and matrix computations. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive applications with moderate CPU demands, making it suitable for in-memory caching, lightweight database tasks, and small-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering solid single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and Intel AMX support for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM on x86_64 architecture
Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 105 MB L3 cache
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 hardware acceleration
Average single-core performance and static web serving throughput
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for in-memory caching and lightweight database workloads
